morphology.summary: A borrowing whose form preserves the idea of a forty-day period.

etymology.summary: The word is connected to Italian quaranta giorni, a forty-day period of isolation.

semantic_drift.summary: The meaning narrows from a forty-day period to disease-related isolation and then broadens to general enforced separation.

developments.description: A fixed duration becomes an infection-control practice.
The term generalizes beyond exact forty-day duration.

present_usage.summary: Modern use covers isolation of people, animals, places, files, or systems to prevent spread or harm.

senses.definition: Isolation or restriction intended to prevent disease transmission.

examples.text: The traveler remained in quarantine until the test results arrived.
examples.note: Original EI usage example for this capsule.

senses.definition: Separation of a risky file, system, or item to prevent damage.

examples.text: The antivirus tool moved the attachment into quarantine.
examples.note: Original EI usage example for this capsule.

cultural_frame.summary: The word carries public-health, border-control, safety, and containment frames.

frames.label: public safety containment
frames.description: Frames separation as protection against contagion or risk.

frames.label: civil restriction
frames.description: Frames separation as an imposed limit on movement or access.

pragmatics.summary: Quarantine can imply care, control, fear, or institutional authority.

stances.label: protective measure
stances.description: Presents isolation as necessary risk reduction.

stances.label: coercive restriction
stances.description: Presents isolation as burdensome or imposed.

expansion.paragraph: Quarantine preserves a historical forty-day frame but no longer requires forty days. Its meaning shifted from a specific waiting period to disease isolation and then to broader containment practices. Pragmatically it can sound protective, coercive, technical, or fearful depending on context.

trace.contribution: The word is connected to Italian quaranta giorni, a forty-day period of isolation.
The meaning narrows from a forty-day period to disease-related isolation and then broadens to general enforced separation.
Quarantine can imply care, control, fear, or institutional authority.

capsule_summary: A forty-days word generalized into disease isolation and broader risk containment.

provenance.citation: Etymonoetic Interlingua curated analysis for quarantine.

provenance.note: Manual layer separation, pragmatic analysis, examples, and expansion.

unresolved_questions: Add public-health history citations beyond dictionary evidence.
